medications: Naproxen as needed for pelvic pain
health.mental_health: High stress and intermittent anxiety related to her teenager’s school suspensions, frequent calls from administrators, and worry about safety; occasional insomnia despite CPAP adherence.
recent_visits: Gynecology follow-up for endometriosis pain management and hormone therapy adjustment (last month)
Sleep medicine telehealth check-in to review CPAP compliance and mask fit (6 weeks ago)
Primary care visit for elevated blood pressure and fatigue labs (2 months ago)
Urgent care visit for migraines after several nights of poor sleep (3 months ago)

finance.debt: Car loan with ~$7,800 remaining; carrying ~$3,200 on a credit card after multiple tutoring/therapy co-pays and replacing a broken laptop for her teen’s schoolwork. She is on a payment plan for a past out-of-network gynecology bill.
investments: High-yield savings account for emergency fund
recent_transactions: After-school tutoring package for algebra and writing support
Replacement smartphone screen repair for teen after an incident at school
Monthly CPAP supplies order (filters and mask cushions)
MetroCard reload and parking fees for school meetings

relationships: Teenage son, Mateo Herrera (ongoing behavioral and attendance issues)
Sister, Ana Herrera (lives in Silver Spring; helps with pickups and occasional supervision)
Close friend and former coworker, Danielle Brooks (primary confidante about parenting stress)
Co-parent/ex-partner, Javier Morales (inconsistent involvement; disputes about discipline and school meetings)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly passive on Facebook and Instagram; shares occasional photos from museums and neighborhood walks. Avoids posting about her son’s school situation; uses a private parent group to read resources without commenting much.
private_conversations: Discussions with school counselor about potential IEP/504 evaluation and behavioral intervention plan
Text thread with her sister about a recent incident involving vaping and suspected marijuana use
Conversations with ex-partner about possible outpatient program and disagreements over consequences
Calls with a trusted friend about fears of police involvement if behavior escalates

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri, 8:30 AM–5:00 PM (occasionally stays until 6:30 PM for sample runs and inventory)
regular_appointments: Weekly child/adolescent therapy session for Mateo (Tuesdays after school)
Monthly check-in call with school case manager/attendance office
CPAP equipment maintenance and compliance check (quarterly)
upcoming_events: School disciplinary hearing and re-entry meeting (next Thursday morning)
Parent-teacher conference for multiple classes (next week)
Sleep clinic follow-up telehealth appointment (in 3 weeks)
Lab safety refresher training and audit walk-through (end of month)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 6:00 AM to manage CPAP cleanup, quick breakfast, and commute by Metro. Workday is structured around bench tasks and timed runs. Evenings are dominated by monitoring homework, checking the school portal, and de-escalating conflicts; she keeps her phone on loud for school calls.

professional.company: Mid-size biotech company in the D.C. metro area focused on immunology assays and clinical research support (contract lab serving academic medical centers)
projects: ELISA and multiplex cytokine assay runs for an immuno-oncology study
Sample accessioning, chain-of-custody documentation, and freezer inventory reconciliation
Preparing buffers/media and maintaining QC logs under GLP-like procedures
Supporting a tech transfer of a qPCR-based assay to a partner site
professional.performance_issues: Has used more unscheduled leave recently for school meetings and urgent pickups; occasional concentration lapses after poor sleep. She worries about being seen as unreliable during an upcoming audit and has asked her supervisor for flexible start times on hearing days.
workplace_relationships: Supportive supervisor, Priya Nair, who approves occasional schedule adjustments but expects advance notice
Tension with a senior scientist, Mark Feldman, who is strict about turnaround times and documentation
Friendly rapport with QA associate, Lila Chen, who helps her stay audit-ready

hobbies: Weekend walks on the National Mall and along the Anacostia River Trail
Smithsonian museum visits for low-cost outings
Cooking simple Latin-inspired meals (soups, rice and beans, roasted chicken)
Light yoga/stretching for pelvic pain management
habits: Uses CPAP nightly and tracks sleep metrics in an app
Keeps a locked medication drawer due to concerns about teen access
Limits alcohol but occasionally has a glass of wine on high-stress nights
Checks school portal and email multiple times per evening
Keeps emergency contacts and crisis resources saved after a recent escalation at home
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in Northwest D.C.; lives with her teenage son. The home environment can be tense, and she has arranged a neighbor as an emergency backup contact for evenings when she is at work late.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a lower-sodium diet for blood pressure; avoids heavy dairy when pelvic symptoms flare; relies on meal prep and quick options during weeks with multiple school meetings.
